summary: Draft, closed-unmerged PR that advanced decompilation of the name-entry/name-list menu code in src/melee/mn/mnname.c and cleaned up related prototypes. The diff replaces multiple mnName placeholders with C implementations, converts many mnname.h declarations from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures, updates persistent-name data access to use struct gmm_x2FF8_inner fields, and adds cross-header declarations for name menu input and persistent-name initialization. Automated decomp.dev feedback reported 3 newly matched mnname functions and 9 partial improvements, but there was no human review text in the slice.
what_changed: Implemented several previously stubbed mnName routines in src/melee/mn/mnname.c, including mnName_8023749C, CreateNameAtIndex, fn_80238540, mnName_GetPageCount, mnName_GetColumnCount, mnName_802388D4, mnName_80238A04, mnName_80239EBC, mnName_80239F5C, mnName_80239FFC, mnName_8023A058, and IsNameNotAllowed.
Reworked GetNameText and IsNameValid to access GetPersistentNameData(slot)->namedata instead of byte-offset arithmetic at +0x198.
Added a concrete prototype for InitializePersistentNameData in gmmain_lib.h: void InitializePersistentNameData(u8 slot).
Added a concrete prototype for mnNameNew_MainInput(void) in mnnamenew.h so fn_80238540 can dispatch to it.

Updated mnname.h to replace many UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with typed returns and parameters, including bool IsNameUnique(char* name), void DeleteName(u8 slot), void CreateNameAtIndex(u8 slot), s32 mnName_GetPageCount(void), s32 mnName_GetColumnCount(void), and bool IsNameNotAllowed(char* name).
Added mnname.h include to mndiagram.c, likely because newly typed declarations became needed across the mn subsystem.
Introduced local inferred structs in mnname.c for menu/JObj data access, including MnName88D4Data and MnNameData, to express offsets like x14, x18, x1C, x24, x30, and x3C.
The PR was closed without merge and marked draft in the GitHub metadata.
smart_moves: Using struct gmm_x2FF8_inner and its namedata field instead of hard-coded +0x198 pointer math made persistent-name logic clearer and improved type evidence for related functions.

CreateNameAtIndex was small but important: it writes the name terminator into namedata[0], sets x1A1 to 1, then calls InitializePersistentNameData(slot), matching the persistent-name initialization flow with a concrete u8 slot parameter.

fn_80238540 models a simple menu-state dispatcher on mn_804A04F0.x10, routing cases to mnName_MainInput, mnNameNew_MainInput, and mnName_ConfirmNameDeleteInput; this exposes the relationship between name-list, new-name, and delete-confirm input modes.

mnName_GetPageCount and mnName_GetColumnCount share the same valid-name counting loop over 0x78 persistent slots but use different page/column divisors, preserving the likely original control-flow style with explicit remainder flags.
IsNameNotAllowed iterates the NotAllowedNamesList until mnNameNew_NullCharacter and uses CompareNameStrings, which cleanly connects banned-name validation with existing string comparison behavior.
The JObj helper functions preserve HSD-style null/assert/flag dirty-matrix behavior rather than over-simplifying it, which is often necessary for matching baselib object manipulation code.
decomp_lessons: When a persistent game data struct is known, replace byte offsets with field access early; it improves downstream signatures and often makes small functions like GetNameText, IsNameValid, and CreateNameAtIndex easier to match.
Typed headers can unlock or stabilize adjacent decompilation: this PR changed prototypes in mnname.h, mnnamenew.h, and gmmain_lib.h alongside C implementations.
Menu dispatch functions can often be expressed directly as switch statements over a global menu state, but the exact return types of the called input functions should be fixed in headers first.

For list scans over sentinel-terminated tables, keep the sentinel variable and casts close to the original code when matching requires it; mnName_8023749C and IsNameNotAllowed both compare against explicit terminator globals.

For UI/JObj code, preserving repeated null checks, assert line numbers, HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub conditions, and HSD_JObjReqAnimAll/HSD_JObjAnimAll sequencing may matter more than writing idiomatic simplified C.
Even partially matched implementations are useful if they establish struct layouts and signatures; the bot report showed many functions improved to about 85–99% despite not all being 100% matches.
naming_conventions: Project-visible name menu APIs use descriptive names where behavior is known: GetNameText, GetNameCount, IsNameListFull, CompareNameStrings, IsNameUnique, DeleteName, IsNameValid, CreateNameAtIndex, IsNameNotAllowed.
Unidentified menu functions remain address-based with the mnName_802xxxxx or fn_802xxxxx convention.

Persistent-name data fields were referenced through inferred struct fields such as namedata and x1A1, while less understood local menu structs retained offset-style names like x14, x18, x1C, x24, x30, and x3C.
HSD object variables use conventional names like HSD_GObj* gobj and HSD_JObj* jobj, with local obj/temp/result variables for child/next traversal.
Globals retain symbol-address style names when semantic names are not yet known, such as mnName_803ED538, mnName_804D4BF4, mnName_804D4BFC, mnNameNew_803EE724, and mnNameNew_803EE720.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used explicit gotos in mnName_8023749C to model loop_cond/loop_body control flow, suggesting matching required preserving a non-idiomatic loop shape.
Used u8 and s8 casts in index and terminator comparisons, including (u8) idx, (u8) arg1, and (s8) terminator, to control signedness and generated code.
Kept explicit remainder variables in page/column count functions instead of replacing them with compact arithmetic, likely to better match original codegen.
Used child/next traversal of HSD_JObj trees in mnName_802388D4 and cleanup helpers, with NULL handling before accessing child or next.
Preserved assert calls with source-line constants in mnName_80239EBC and mnName_80239F5C, plus the HSD_JObj flag tests before HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub.

Automated matching report showed exact matches for IsNameNotAllowed, CreateNameAtIndex, and mnName_8023A058, and near-matches for functions including fn_80238540, mnName_GetPageCount, mnName_GetColumnCount, mnName_8023749C, mnName_802388D4, mnName_80238A04, mnName_80239EBC, mnName_80239F5C, and mnName_80239FFC.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review records were present in the slice.
decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code improving by +0.01% / +316 bytes.
decomp.dev bot reported 3 new 100% matches: IsNameNotAllowed (+116 bytes), CreateNameAtIndex (+100 bytes), and mnName_8023A058 (+100 bytes).
decomp.dev bot reported 9 improved but still unmatched mnname functions, with after percentages ranging from about 85.72% to 99.17%.
follow_up_queries: Check whether later PRs merged or reworked the mnName functions from closed draft PR #2079.
Search for subsequent stabilization of struct gmm_x2FF8_inner fields, especially namedata and x1A1.
Look for later reviews or merged commits involving mnName_GetPageCount, mnName_GetColumnCount, and mnName_8023749C to see how the near-matches were completed.
Investigate whether local structs MnName88D4Data and MnNameData were later moved to shared headers or renamed with semantic field names.
Search for follow-up changes to mnNameNew_MainInput and InitializePersistentNameData prototypes to confirm final accepted signatures.
